summary: Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to a ceasefire covering all of Lebanon, with both sides pledging not to attack each other. Israeli forces continue targeted strikes in southern Lebanon, including the killing of a Hezbollah missile‑unit commander and interception of multiple rockets and drones. Regional actors, including Iran and the United States, are issuing threats and adjusting military postures amid the ongoing hostilities.
key_points: Hezbollah pledged not to fire rockets at Israel, and Israel pledged not to attack Beirut, with the ceasefire to apply to all of Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/61647).
Israeli air force killed a Hezbollah missile‑unit commander, Mahmad Musa Matarik, responsible for hundreds of rocket and UAV launches (Source: @idf_telegram/24083).
One Hezbollah rocket launched toward Upper Galilee was intercepted, and an interceptor missile was fired at a suspected Hezbollah drone over Kiryat Shmona (Source: @manniefabian/58352).
Two rockets aimed at Safed were intercepted, triggering sirens in numerous towns but causing no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58356).
Iran warned northern Israeli residents to evacuate if Israel attacks Beirut, and threatened attacks on Israel should it strike Beirut (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3230; @lelotsenzura/92414).
U.S. Central Command reported thousands of service members supporting a blockade against Iran, redirecting 121 commercial vessels and disabling five (Source: @shin_persian/6362).
President Trump announced a ceasefire in Lebanon after talks with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and Hezbollah, stating no Israeli forces would enter Beirut (Source: @yediotnews25/130323).

sections.executive_summary: Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to a ceasefire covering all of Lebanon, with both sides pledging not to attack each other. Israeli forces continue targeted strikes in southern Lebanon, including the killing of a Hezbollah missile‑unit commander and interception of multiple rockets and drones. Regional actors, including Iran and the United States, are issuing threats and adjusting military postures amid the ongoing hostilities.

points: Iranian officials warned residents of northern Israel to evacuate if Israel attacks Beirut, stating the warning applies if Israel bombs Beirut or Dahiyeh (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3230).
Iranian leaders declared they would not tolerate escalation in Lebanon and emphasized control of the Strait of Hormuz, linking it to regional stability (Source: @presstv/192257).
Iranian media reported that Iran would attack Israel if Israel attacks Beirut, echoing earlier threats (Source: @lelotsenzura/92414).
Iranian strikes have reportedly damaged at least 20 U.S. military sites, according to satellite analysis (Source: @presstv/192265).
The IDF announced the killing of Hezbollah missile‑unit commander Mahmad Musa Matarik, who oversaw hundreds of rocket and UAV launches toward Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24083).
Israeli military intercepted a Hezbollah rocket over Upper Galilee and engaged a suspected Hezbollah drone over Kiryat Shmona; results are under review (Source: @manniefabian/58352).
Prime Minister Netanyahu stated Israel will continue deep operations in southern Lebanon to eliminate Hezbollah threats and keep northern Israeli communities safe (Source: @almogboker78/17248).
The IDF reported multiple hostile aircraft intrusions and rocket launches from Lebanon, some intercepted and others landing in open areas, with no injuries reported (Source: @idf_telegram/24074).
The IDF reported the elimination of a Hamas operative who served as a doctor in a southern Gaza hospital, responsible for planning attacks against Israeli forces (Source: @idf_telegram/24072).
No direct Gaza‑related incidents were detailed in the provided messages beyond the Hamas operative elimination (Source: @idf_telegram/24072).
Hezbollah announced it will not fire rockets at Israel, and Israel pledged not to attack Beirut, with the ceasefire to apply across Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/61647).
Three militants were killed in a vehicle attack in Majdal, southern Lebanon, prior to a Trump announcement (Source: @lelotsenzura/92425).
Israeli forces continue strikes in southern Lebanon, targeting Hezbollah positions from the Dahieh area to the town of Sour (Source: @idf_telegram/24078).
German and Norwegian foreign ministers cancelled trips to Lebanon due to the risk of Israeli attacks (Source: @SharghDaily/163321).
No specific Syrian or Iraqi operational details were present in the source messages.
U.S. Central Command reported thousands of U.S. service members supporting a blockade against Iran, with 121 commercial vessels redirected and five disabled (Source: @shin_persian/6362).
No other Gulf state actions were detailed in the provided messages.

key_developments: Hezbollah pledged not to fire rockets at Israel, and Israel pledged not to attack Beirut, with the ceasefire covering all of Lebanon (Source: @amitsegal/61647).
Israeli air force eliminated Hezbollah missile‑unit commander Mahmad Musa Matarik, linked to hundreds of rocket and UAV launches (Source: @idf_telegram/24083).
A Hezbollah rocket launched toward Upper Galilee was intercepted, and an interceptor missile engaged a suspected Hezbollah drone over Kiryat Shmona (Source: @manniefabian/58352).
Two rockets aimed at Safed were intercepted, triggering sirens but causing no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58356).
Iran warned northern Israeli residents to evacuate if Israel attacks Beirut and threatened retaliation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3230; @lelotsenzura/92414).
U.S. Central Command reported thousands of service members supporting a blockade against Iran, redirecting 121 commercial vessels and disabling five (Source: @shin_persian/6362).
President Trump announced a ceasefire in Lebanon after talks with Netanyahu and Hezbollah, stating no Israeli forces would be sent to Beirut (Source: @yediotnews25/130323).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains capable of launching rockets and drones, as evidenced by multiple interceptions, but the declared ceasefire may limit large‑scale attacks. Iran's explicit threats to strike northern Israel if Beirut is hit raise the risk of a broader regional escalation. U.S. naval and air assets are actively enforcing a blockade against Iran, indicating sustained pressure that could provoke retaliatory actions.
sections.regional_response: Israel is maintaining a proactive strike posture in southern Lebanon while publicly committing to the ceasefire, aiming to neutralize Hezbollah's launch sites. Iran is leveraging diplomatic warnings and limited kinetic actions to deter Israeli moves, while the United States continues to project power through its blockade and vessel interdictions. Diplomatic channels involving the U.S., Israel, and Hezbollah appear fragile, with high‑level talks referenced but no concrete de‑escalation guarantees.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah rocket and drone launch patterns around the Upper Galilee and Safed areas should be intensified, given recent interception activity. The death of a senior Hezbollah missile commander may temporarily disrupt launch coordination but could also trigger retaliatory attacks. Iranian threat communications suggest possible targeting of northern Israeli civilian areas if hostilities expand, warranting civilian evacuation planning. U.S. blockade enforcement indicates a sustained focus on curbing Iranian maritime capabilities, which may affect supply lines to proxy groups in the region.
